master_row.title: Megastructure Formation Theorem
master_row.canonical_statement: Standing-wave interference across the universe produces filaments with wavelength \(\lambda=\pi R_{\mathrm{universe}}/n\), where \(R_{\mathrm{universe}}\) is the Hubble radius. For the selected mode scale, this gives \(\lambda\approx150\,\mathrm{Mpc}\). These filaments are modeled as flux tubes with \(T^2\) topology, and the predicted spacing is compared against observed cosmic-web scales.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a fourier/spectral transform relating π, R_, n, F, T.
master_row.dictionary: Standing wave interference across the universe produces filaments with wavelength λ = π R_universe / n, where R_universe is the Hubble radius.
master_row.encyclopedia: Technical interpretation remains tied to the local registry and equation witness. Interpretive role: This entry takes the toroidal motion axiom and lifts it to cosmological structure. It says that the same wrapped topology that shaped motion can also shape the spacing of the cosmic web.
